NCT04659161.name: EMERGENT-2
NCT04659161.group: Acute schizophrenia exacerbation, KarXT 125/30 mg PO BID vs placebo (5 wk, PANSS total change)

allOutcomes.title: PANSS total score change from baseline at week 5 (primary, MD)

NCT04659161.snippet: Source: Kaul I et al. Lancet 2024;403:160-170 (EMERGENT-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 252 adults aged 18-65 with DSM-5 schizophrenia currently experiencing acute psychotic exacerbation (PANSS total >=80, PANSS positive subscale score >=4 on at least 3 items) randomized 1:1 double-blind to KarXT (xanomeline 125 mg + trospium 30 mg) orally twice daily (n=126) or matched placebo (n=126) for 5 weeks of inpatient treatment.

evidence.label: Primary - PANSS Total at Week 5
evidence.text: PANSS total score change from baseline at week 5: KarXT -21.2 vs placebo -11.6; estimated treatment difference MD -9.6 points (95% CI -13.9 to -5.2; P<0.0001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - PANSS Positive / Negative / CGI-S
evidence.text: PANSS positive subscale ETD -2.9 (P<0.0001). PANSS negative subscale ETD -1.8 (P=0.006). CGI-Severity ETD -0.6 (P<0.0001). Safety: nausea, dyspepsia, constipation, vomiting more common with KarXT; no weight gain, no extrapyramidal symptoms, no prolactin elevation (distinct advantage over D2 antagonists).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by site. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 77% completion (typical for inpatient antipsychotic trials). D4 LOW - PANSS raters centrally certified. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 65 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Subject is aged 18 to 65 years, inclusive, at screening.~2. Subject is capable of providing informed consent.~   1. A signed informed consent form must be provided before any study assessments are performed.~  2. Subject must be fluent (oral and written) in English to consent~3. Subject has a primary diagnosis of schizophrenia established by a comprehensive psychiatric evaluation based on the DSM-5 criteria and confirmed by Mini International Neuropsychiatric Interview fo...

NCT04738123.name: EMERGENT-3
NCT04738123.group: Acute schizophrenia exacerbation, KarXT vs placebo (5 wk, replicate pivotal)

allOutcomes.title: PANSS total score change from baseline at week 5 (primary, MD)

NCT04738123.snippet: Source: Kaul I et al. JAMA Psychiatry 2024;81:749-756 (EMERGENT-3).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 256 adults with DSM-5 schizophrenia in acute exacerbation (PANSS total >=80) randomized 1:1 double-blind to KarXT orally twice daily (n=128) or matched placebo (n=128) for 5 weeks inpatient. EMERGENT-3 was the replicate pivotal trial to EMERGENT-2.

evidence.label: Primary - PANSS Total at Week 5
evidence.text: PANSS total score change from baseline at week 5: KarXT -20.6 vs placebo -12.2; estimated treatment difference MD -8.4 points (95% CI -12.4 to -4.3; P<0.0001). Primary endpoint met, replicating EMERGENT-2.

evidence.label: Secondary - Safety Profile
evidence.text: Cholinergic TEAEs (nausea 19%, dyspepsia 16%, vomiting 14%) and anticholinergic effects (constipation 20%, dry mouth) more common on KarXT. No significant weight gain, no EPS, no prolactin increase, no QTc prolongation signal. Consistent safety profile with EMERGENT-2.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 73% completion. D4 LOW - central PANSS certification. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 65 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Subject is aged 18 to 65 years, inclusive, at screening.~2. Subject is capable of providing informed consent.~   1. A signed informed consent form must be provided before any study assessments are performed.~  2. Subject must be fluent (oral and written) in English or local language to consent~3. Subject has a primary diagnosis of schizophrenia established by a comprehensive psychiatric evaluation based on the DSM-5 criteria and confirmed by Mini International Neuropsychi...

title: RapidMeta Psychiatry | KarXT (Xanomeline-Trospium) for Schizophrenia v1.1
